task_objective: Wait for asynchronous SIGSTOP delivery before accepting the pre-scope launch gate, require a full thread-group stop, and bind long-lived stop-only leases to process identity.
tests_added: A delayed synthetic proc state reproduces R-to-T signal delivery and proves acquisition waits instead of checking immediately.

Deterministic fake-clock coverage verifies immediate T, R-to-T convergence, strict timeout, D, Z, ptrace t with TracerPid, unreadable and disappearing stat files, staggered multi-thread stops, and an empty task list.
Injected monotonic and wait callbacks are observed directly, while an immediate full stop proves wait is not called.
Linux real-process integration coverage verifies SIGSTOP against single- and multi-threaded child processes with nested cleanup.
Stop-only lease verification, renewal, and release accept a matching identity and reject a replaced start_ticks value without sending SIGCONT.
design_decisions: The waiter polls only for the state transition caused by SIGSTOP; scope discovery remains a single attempt because SIGSTOP prevents Steam scope creation.
Only uppercase T is accepted, and every task stat must report T before the child scan runs.
The 100ms timeout and 0.5ms poll interval are named constants based on measured 0.16-0.87ms Steam Deck delivery, not a claimed scheduler guarantee.
Stop-only leases retain LaunchProcessIdentity and skip SIGCONT when pid, owner uid, or start ticks no longer match.

The old test_acquirer_constructor_does_not_expose_dead_polling_hooks test was deleted because it incorrectly prohibited the live clock injection required here. It was replaced with an assertion that injected wait is actually called; the existing discover_calls == 1 assertion continues to guard against forbidden scope polling.
The shared launch-gate scope fixture now writes per-task stat state because the production invariant intentionally strengthened from leader-only to all threads.
Identity and release helpers remain in watchdog_lease.py so watchdog.py stays within its enforced 370-line maintainability budget.
mutation_results: Replacing the bounded waiter with an immediate _is_stopped check made test_scope_not_ready_waits_for_sigstop_delivery fail with state=R.
Ignoring the injected wait made test_acquirer_uses_injected_wait_until_sigstop_is_delivered fail because the observed wait list was empty.
Accepting only the leader's T state made test_wait_until_stopped_requires_every_thread_to_stop fail because no poll occurred while the sibling remained R.
Dropping identity verification from verify_gate made test_stop_only_lease_rejects_reused_pid_and_does_not_send_sigcont fail because the replaced PID was incorrectly accepted.

results.automated: Both atomic implementation commits passed repository protocol hooks: ruff, formatting, ty, 818 Python tests at 88.51% coverage, 287 frontend tests, TypeScript checking, and frontend build/package validation.
results.on_device: Deferred and outstanding. A Steam Deck run must first verify the installed build version, then confirm the black-screen conflict hold, restore-before-start behavior, cancellation resume, plugin-unload resume, and expected launch-gate log messages. A green synthetic and real-child suite is not evidence that the complete Steam launch path works on device.
